t_ch: …你想起克洛多梅尔早已背叛了你——他以人性与数以亿计的生命为代价，自军团手中换得苟活的机会…你想起希尔德贝尼娅，想起洛塔尼娅……
t_en: ...You remember Chlodomer had already betrayed you. He exchanged his humanity and millions of lives with the Legion for his own survival... Then, you think about Hildebenia, and Chlodania...

t_ch: 你想起这宴席临近尾声。是的…就快要到那位绝灭大君给定的最后期限了。「大人…我们是否应该*归顺*它们？」你的侍从将那可恨的通牒呈上。
t_en: You realize the banquet is coming to an end. Indeed... the deadline handed down by that Lord Ravager is fast approaching. "My lord... should we SURRENDER to them?" Your attendant presents you with that despicable warning notice.

t_ch: 「是时候结束这一切了…」你正想提笔，却见虚卒如浪潮般破门而入。
t_en: "It's time to end this..." As you are about to pick up your pen, hoards of Voidrangers break down the doors and pour into the hall.

t_ch: 走开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 空气中悬浮着一丛气泡，七彩的光华流溢其中，恍惚间形成似有深意的画面。靠近时，一个像是黑塔的女声不带感情地低语：振翅。
t_en: Bubbles are floating in the air with a colorful glow gliding on their surfaces. For some reason, they form an image that seems to carry a profound meaning. As you approach them, a female voice that sounds similar to Herta's murmurs monotonously, "Flapping wings."

t_ch: 触碰忆泡。
t_en: Touch the Memory Bubble.

t_ch: 你靠近忆泡，感到一丝凉意。凉意温柔地穿过你的皮肤、骨肉，穿过更深层的……
t_en: You approach the bubble and experience a chilly sensation that gently pierces through your skin, flesh, and bones, then goes down deeper...

t_ch: 你竟意外地发现，自己正受困于一阴暗逼仄的空间。空气中潮气弥漫，含混着木制品霉烂后特有的腐败气息。
t_en: To your surprise, you are stuck in a dark and cramped room. The air is thick and dank, mixed with the putrid scene unique to mouldy, rotten wood.

t_ch: 你想要转身，活动自己紧绷僵滞的关节，却发现自己动弹不得——这不光是因为你根本无处施展，更因为你周身肌腱早已因极端恐惧僵直、战抖。
t_en: You want to turn around to exercise your tight joints, but you find yourself unable to move — not only is there no space around you, but your muscles and tendons are also stiff and trembling out of abject fear.

t_ch: 因为你已经听得足够清晰…在这紧锁的木匣之外，正有为数众多遭到诅咒的生物四处嗅探：那栉齿样的触角正急躁地四处抽打，鞭笞着与你仅距咫尺之遥的一切……
t_en: It's because you can clearly hear them... those multitudes of cursed creatures sniffing around outside this locked wooden box. Their serrated feelers are impatiently thrashing around, lashing out at everything outside of your narrow confines...

t_ch: 你听到它们正在室内横冲直撞，疯狂穿行…你听到那如铁钩般的肢足与钢甲似的甲壳正躁动不止，发出令人发狂的锐响……
t_en: You can hear them dashing madly around the room... you can hear the constant movements of their iron-hooked limbs and armor-like carapace, producing a piercing noise that drives one into insanity...

t_ch: 你当然很明白…这些该死的节肢生物根本没有沟通的智能！那副垂着粘涎的恶心口器本该只用于咀嚼进食——但你确信你听到了……
t_en: Of course you know these despicable arthropods have no intellect to communicate with you! Their disgusting mouthpieces, dripping with sticky saliva, should only be used for chewing and eating — but you are sure you heard something...

t_ch: 它们…毫无疑问正在低语着什么——可那副口器…不，是那副*该死的*口器…本不该发出这样的声音…！
t_en: They... are definitely whispering something — but their mouthpieces... no, those DARN mouthpieces... should not be able to make that kind of sound!

t_ch: 难道是那覆着油膜的、令人作呕的鞘翅吗？你侧耳倾听…果然，正是那对恶心的翅膀正煽动空气——反复地、尖锐地、喑哑地嘶鸣着那受诅咒的*名字*……
t_en: Could the sound come from their filthy wings covered with an oily film? You listen intently... yes, the disgusting wings are flapping in the air as they repeatedly utter that cursed NAME in a shrill and coarse hiss...

t_ch: 塔伊兹育罗斯…塔伊兹█育罗斯…塔█伊兹█育罗斯…塔█伊兹█ ██育█罗█ █斯…塔█ ██ 伊█ █兹育███ █罗█斯███……
t_en: Tayzzyronth... Tayzzy█ronth... Ta█yzzy█ronth... Ta█yzz██y█ronth█... Ta█... █yzzy███ron█th███...

t_ch: 走开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 空气中悬浮着一丛气泡，七彩的光华流溢其中，恍惚间形成似有深意的画面。靠近时，一个像是黑塔的女声不带感情地低语：回家的路。
t_en: Bubbles are floating in the air with a colorful glow gliding on their surfaces. For some reason, they form an image that seems to carry a profound meaning. As you approach them, a female voice that sounds similar to Herta's murmurs monotonously, "The way back home."

t_ch: 触碰忆泡。
t_en: Touch the Memory Bubble.

t_ch: 你靠近忆泡，感到一丝凉意。凉意温柔地穿过你的皮肤、骨肉，穿过更深层的……
t_en: You approach the bubble and experience a chilly sensation that gently pierces through your skin, flesh, and bones, then goes down deeper...

t_ch: 今天对你来说应当是个好日子——就在刚才，你那总是愁容满面的部门主管将你的公司评级提升至P<unbreak>30</unbreak>了。
t_en: Today should have been your lucky day — your department head, whose face is always as long as a fiddle, just raised your IPC rating to P<unbreak>30</unbreak>.

t_ch: 你转告了远在家乡的母亲，通讯器那头的她听起来很是激动：你似乎听到了她黏质的身体发出咕嘟咕嘟的声音——这是你们巴维鲁人喜悦时特有的生理反应。
t_en: You share the news with your mother living in your faraway hometown, and her voice sounds excited on the other end of the communication device. You seem to hear her slimy body making a bubbling sound — a special physiological reaction of your Baviru race in moments of happiness.

t_ch: 你切断通讯，跨上那崭新的浮空摩托。这大宝贝可花了你整整十八个月的工资——虽然贵，但它能治愈你在职场中受到的创伤。你觉得值，可惜今天疗效不大好。
t_en: You end the call and get on your brand-new floating motorcycle. This baby costs <unbreak>18</unbreak> months of your salary — although it is pricey, it can soothe the trauma you have suffered in your workplace. You think it is worth its price tag, but it doesn't do much to your pain today.

t_ch: 该不该告诉老母亲真相？这「传统项目部」——分部——其实只是个养老部门，根本毫无出路？你呜咕，嗡嗡，拧死油门，蹿入新伦蒂尼恩的空中立交航道。
t_en: Should you tell your mother the truth? That the Traditional Project Department is, in fact, a place for people to retire in and has no future? With a whimper, you twist the throttle to the max and dash into the aerial interchange lane in New Londinium.

t_ch: 你喜欢在立交航道上飙车。在这里，新伦蒂尼恩看起来就像生日矿石糕上的烛光——闪亮，渺小，一吹即熄。你觉得工作上的不如意根本不算什么了。
t_en: You like joyriding along the interchange lane. From here, the city looks like the tiny flickering candlelights on a mineral birthday cake, easily blown out. The troubles you face at work all fade away at that moment.

t_ch: 路上没什么人。你可以短暂地抬头看看云幕投影新闻，看看这座忙不迭的城市又发生了什么。原来是那位联席总理。「好吧。」你咕啾，决定还是专注于路况。
t_en: The streets are deserted. You look up briefly at the news projected in the sky to find out the latest happenings in this bustling city. The face of the co-prime minister appears on the screen. "Fine," you grumble, deciding to focus on the road ahead.

t_ch: 可惜晚了。就在你将注意力从云幕扯回道路的瞬间，浮空摩托突然哑火，不受控制地坠向地面…你只希望不要摔进白金汉大道，不然随后的罚款可够你喝一壶了。
t_en: Yet it is too late. The instant you return your attention from the clouds to the road, the engine of the floating motorcycle suddenly misfires and plummets to the ground out of your control... You just hope you won't land on Buckingham Avenue, or you will be fined an enormous amount.

t_ch: 还好，附近只是门可罗雀的老商业街，你没有砸到任何人。谈笑风生的老爷夫人们先是吓了一跳，发现你不过是坨湿答答的黏液后，复又畅快地碰起杯来。
t_en: To your relief, there is only an old shopping street with few pedestrians nearby, and your landing has hurt no one. Elderly couples who were happily chatting suffer a fright, but as soon as they realize you are nothing but a puddle of slime, they immediately clink their glasses in happiness again.

t_ch: 「真该死！」你咕噜咕噜，盯着碾作一团、火花四溅的摩托残骸，不知如何是好。这里离公寓还有多远？你毫无头绪，只好仰望天空，想要问问群星的意见。
t_en: "I can't believe this!" You bubble as you stare at the motorcycle reduced into a pile of burning wreckage, not knowing what to do. You have no idea how far you are from your condo. You can only look up at the sky and ask for advice from the stars.

t_ch: ——可你只听星星们在云幕间如是说：「即使是新伦蒂尼恩东区最贫穷的工人，想到我们与公司共同拥有的财富，都会不由自主地挺起胸膛！」……
t_en: But you hear the stars in the sky saying, "Even the poorest worker in east New Londinium can't help but feel proud of the wealth we jointly own with the IPC!"

t_ch: 走开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 空气中悬浮着一丛气泡，七彩的光华流溢其中，恍惚间形成似有深意的画面。靠近时，一个像是黑塔的女声不带感情地低语：全然蒸发。
t_en: Bubbles are floating in the air with a colorful glow gliding on their surfaces. For some reason, they form an image that seems to carry a profound meaning. As you approach them, a female voice that sounds similar to Herta's murmurs monotonously, "Complete disappearance."

t_ch: 触碰忆泡。
t_en: Touch the Memory Bubble.

t_ch: 你靠近忆泡，感到一丝凉意。凉意温柔地穿过你的皮肤、骨肉，穿过更深层的……
t_en: You approach the bubble and experience a chilly sensation that gently pierces through your skin, flesh, and bones, then goes down deeper...

t_ch: 尽管驻留在星球地面的博识学会民俗学团队还未回复任何讯息，但你们的飞行器的燃料已近乎消耗殆尽。「我们必须降落！」驾驶员在通讯频道里宣布。
t_en: The folklore team of the Intelligentsia Guild stationed on the planet has not replied, but your aircraft is running out of fuel. "We must land now!" The pilot announces through the communication channel.

t_ch: 仪表盘显示你们所处的海拔正在不断下降。现在已经是三位数了。你见舷窗外的风景从云层乱流变为华美屋宇的穹顶，确认了这一事实。
t_en: The dashboard shows your altitude has been decreasing rapidly, dropping to a three-digit figure. The view outside has changed from wispy clouds to the roofs of exquisite houses, confirming the descent is taking place.

t_ch: 「还是检测不到任何通讯吗？」你的队长显得有些烦躁。可在你看来，学会的人不过是一群打着学术幌子的经费骗子罢了！何必这样担忧？你不理解。
t_en: "Still no signals detected?" Your captain sounds anxious. In your view, people from the Guild are swindlers who hoodwink others under the guise of knowledge. You do not understand why the captain is so worried.

t_ch: 「全频带阻塞，和七天前一样，」书呆子的声音依旧不带感情，「而且，没有在任何居住区——不，是任何区域——检测到生体热量排放。」
t_en: "All frequency bands are blocked, just like a week ago." The nerd speaks in his usual flat tone. "Also, no heat emission of living organisms are detected in the residential areas — or rather, anywhere."

t_ch: 「以防你们听不懂——这实际上意味着，星球表面的所有活物全部*蒸发*了。」他补充，扶了扶眼镜。虽然这家伙的态度总让你不爽，你想，但这可真是稀奇。
t_en: "In case you don't understand what this means, I'm saying all living organisms on the surface of the planet have EVAPORATED." He adds while pushing his glasses upward. The nerd's attitude always gets on your nerves, but you still can't help feeling incredulous at this.

t_ch: 星球表面的所有活物全部消失？这真的可能吗？或许吧，你揣测。毕竟这群人形真菌忠诚于那样怪异的信仰——没准是它们的神把人全接走了呢！
t_en: All living organisms on the planet have vanished? Is that even possible? Actually, it might be. After all, these humanoid fungi are pious believers of that bizarre faith. Perhaps, their god has brought them all to another place!

t_ch: 你想着，被飞行器底部传来的剧烈震动吓了一跳。看来是落地了——接下来只要找到学会的那帮混球，溜之大吉…你拿起射线枪，跟着队长翻出舱门。
t_en: While being absorbed in your thoughts, you are jolted back to reality by a violent shudder from the bottom of the aircraft. It seems like you have landed. Now, you just need to look for the rascals from the Guild, then get out of here... After picking up a ray gun, you hop out of the hatch after the captain.

t_ch: 你们穿入建筑物后复又穿出…你们搜索了太久，却仍未摆脱困惑：正如书呆子所说——这颗星球表面的一切生命活动，确实以一种诡异的方式戛然而止……
t_en: You and the rest enter a building and exit again... The search lasts for a long time, but your confusion is not answered. As the nerd puts it, all life activities on this planet have creepily come to an abrupt halt.

t_ch: 食物被留在餐盘中却未腐败；无人照管的炉火引发了火灾，复又被大雨熄灭；钢笔在无常的引力中滚落地面，墨水污染了整个房间……
t_en: Food left on plates hasn't rotten yet. Unattended fireplaces set the building on fire which is later put out by rain. Pens drop to the floor due to gravity, and their ink stain the entire room...

t_ch: 唯一未受影响的只有那繁复的纹章。它兀自恒常地从各处注视着真菌文明缔造的一切——街道两侧、衣柜里、旗帜上、壁龛中…*无处不在*。
t_en: The only thing unaffected is the intricate coat of arms that constantly watches over everything created by the fungi civilization from every corner of the world — from both sides of the streets, inside closets, on flags, in niches... it is EVERYWHERE.

t_ch: 走开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 空气中悬浮着一丛气泡，七彩的光华流溢其中，恍惚间形成似有深意的画面。靠近时，一个像是黑塔的女声不带感情地低语：偷窥。
t_en: Bubbles are floating in the air with a colorful glow gliding on their surfaces. For some reason, they form an image that seems to carry a profound meaning. As you approach them, a female voice that sounds similar to Herta's murmurs monotonously, "Peeping."

t_ch: 触碰忆泡。
t_en: Touch the Memory Bubble.

t_ch: 你靠近忆泡，感到一丝凉意。凉意温柔地穿过你的皮肤、骨肉，穿过更深层的……
t_en: You approach the bubble and experience a chilly sensation that gently pierces through your skin, flesh, and bones, then goes down deeper...

t_ch: 亚伯南安号列车上总是如此闷热。你每日下班都与各种机械义体肩挨着肩站在一起，仿佛你冰箱里塞满的台伯鱼罐头，还有罐头里塞满的台伯鱼。
t_en: It is always stiflingly hot on the Abelane Express. Every day, You are squeezed shoulder to shoulder with various mechanical bodies as you commute home from work, as if you are one of those cans of Tiber fish stuffed in your fridge — or a fish inside the cans itself.

t_ch: 你正用后脑勺那颗浑浊的义眼窥视着身后的金发女人，或者说，女机器人。你盯着她的手机屏幕，欣赏着那如同连续剧一般的情感故事。
t_en: You are using the cloudy prosthetic eye at the back of your head to peep at the blonde woman — or, more precisely, the blonde female robot — behind you. You stare at her phone screen, happily browsing her romantic life that reads like a soap opera.

t_ch: 这是一个极度微妙的距离。你称自己为「边缘*道德*行者」。出于你所剩无几的良心，你刻意把正面的目光移开——绝不是因为这样更方便偷窥，绝不是。
t_en: This distance is extremely delicate. You consider yourself as someone who walks on the edge of MORALITY. Out of the remaining shreds of your conscience, you deliberately move your frontal vision away — that's totally not because it's easier to peep this way. Totally not.

t_ch: 她会不会和你是同一个部门的？你不知道，但你喜欢看她和那十八个情人周旋的故事；你也不知道自己和她到底谁更有罪，但你向来有充分的理由自证清白——
t_en: You wonder whether she is a colleague from your department? You have no idea, but you enjoy reading about her interactions and schemes with her <unbreak>18</unbreak> lovers. Between you and her, you are not sure who is more guilty, but you've always had a knack for finding excuses to prove your innocence...

t_ch: 「工作太累，生活没劲，口袋里的亚伯南安星币不够买一张坐票，」你总是这样宽恕自己，「要是我有钱每天坐浮空计程车，哪儿还有机会干这事？」
t_en: "Work is hard, life is dull, and I don't even have enough Abelane Coins to buy tickets with a seat." That's what you've always used to excuse yourself. "I wouldn't behave like this if I can afford to take a floating cab everyday!"

t_ch: 反正都是公司的错——都怪他们搞的什么贸易扩张…你义愤填膺。尽管你还不大明白「贸易扩张」是什么意思，但你记得主管就爱说这个。
t_en: Anyway, it's the IPC's fault for carrying out this so-called trade expansion! You are indignant, but you don't really understand what "trade expansion" even means. You just remember the term is often mentioned by your supervisor.

t_ch: 道德模范谁爱当谁当，反正没钱你不当——你又将目光重新聚焦于那不断刷新滚动的手机屏幕。她正在打字…你聚精会神，努力辨识对话框中的内容：
t_en: Whoever wants to be a saint can be one. You ain't gonna be one unless the act gives you money — you re-focus your gaze on the constantly refreshing phone screen. She is typing... You concentrate and try to read the messages:

t_ch: 「我旁边这家伙，第十九个。」
t_en: "The guy next to me is the <unbreak>19</unbreak>th."

t_ch: 你感觉自己的脑机温度急剧上升。你拼命转动义眼，左看右看，发现四周根本没人——那第十九个是谁？难道你已经暴露了吗？附近有没有巡警？或者……
t_en: You can feel a sharp rise in the temperature of your brain computer. You roll your prosthetic eye around to check your surroundings, but there is no one around you. Who is the <unbreak>19</unbreak>th? Have you been caught? Is there a police patrolling nearby? Or...

t_ch: 你不敢继续往下想了——无论结果是好是坏。你发现自己比想象中还要胆小…体内的电子元件也在高温催化下开始产生化学反应。你感到晕眩。
t_en: Regardless of the outcome, you dare not continue your train of thought. You realize you are timider than you imagined yourself to be... and the electronic components in your body are also kicking off chemical reactions from the catalytic heat. You feel dizzy.

t_ch: 走开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 空气中悬浮着一丛气泡，七彩的光华流溢其中，恍惚间形成似有深意的画面。靠近时，一个像是黑塔的女声不带感情地低语：蹑手蹑脚的爱。
t_en: Bubbles are floating in the air with a colorful glow gliding on their surfaces. For some reason, they form an image that seems to carry a profound meaning. As you approach them, a female voice that sounds similar to Herta's murmurs monotonously, "Cautious love."

t_ch: 触碰忆泡。
t_en: Touch the Memory Bubble.

t_ch: 你靠近忆泡，感到一丝凉意。凉意温柔地穿过你的皮肤、骨肉，穿过更深层的……
t_en: You approach the bubble and experience a chilly sensation that gently pierces through your skin, flesh, and bones, then goes down deeper...

t_ch: 你无法动弹——因你的双手被尖桩钉在了那*丑陋*的巨幅肖像画上，身躯悬垂，被引力扯向地面，摇摇欲坠而不得。你的脚下堆积浸油的柴薪。疼痛令你感到麻木。
t_en: You can't move — both of your hands are staked onto the UGLY and massive portrait. Your body is hanging in midair, dragged towards the ground by gravity, yet unable to fall. Below is a pile of firewood soaked in oil. The excruciating pain is numbing.

t_ch: 「你被指控领导叛乱，亵渎司美之神——那赐予我等头脑与感官的无上存在，恩•德芮，」那就于高座的男人趾高气昂，「对此， 你是否有任何异议？」
t_en: "You are accused of leading a rebellion and desecrating the god of beauty, Anne Duray, who bestowed our minds and senses upon us as blessings." The man sitting on a high seat haughtily states. "Do you have any objections?"

t_ch: 你啐了一口，顺势吐出那卡在喉咙里的、裹着血水的碎牙。什么恩•德芮…你很清楚这名字从何流变而来——伊德莉拉——那*无比丑陋*的存在……
t_en: You spit, getting rid of the bloody broken teeth stuck in your throat. Anne Duray — you know very well where that name is derived from. It's Idrila, that HIDEOUS being...

t_ch: 他们疯狂地崇拜祂，感激祂的恩赐。可这群人竟真以为这世上存在绝对的「美」？何等愚昧！这世间根本不存在完美之物——对于这一点，你再清楚不过。
t_en: The people fanatically worship THEM and are grateful for THEIR gifts, but do these people really believe there is absolute beauty in the world? How foolish! Nothing is perfect, and you understand that better than anyone else.

t_ch: 你想起了哈尔博士，想起自己喜欢偷看他的脸。你想起他在课后蒲公英满天飞的草坪上，拷问你哈洛-Ⅵ于每年何时回归开氏点——那是卫星距欧文利最近的时刻。
t_en: You think of Dr. Howl and how you used to sneak looks at his face. You reminisce about how, at the field filled with dandelions after class, he quizzed you on when Harlow-VI reaches perigee every year — that would be when the satellite is closest to Owenley.

t_ch: 你忘记了日期，又不知道哈洛-Ⅵ恰巧在那天离欧文利最近，而这导致卫星热力辐射使你的脸颊滚烫。他又向你传述星空生态学派的研究……
t_en: You forgot about the date and had no idea that Harlow-VI was in fact closest to Owenley on that day, and the satellite's thermal radiation made your cheeks burn red. Meanwhile, Dr. Howl continued to tell you about the research of Astral Ecology School...

t_ch: …可你的心里没有研究。你只是聆听他的皮肤与布料摩擦所发出的音调，试图用这些音调谱曲；后来，你仔细观察着他衬衫因肌腱折叠产生的褶皱走势……
t_en: ...But you could not think of research. Instead, you listened to the sound produced when his skin brushed the fabric of his shirt, and tried to compose a song with it. Later, you started observing the creases on his shirt formed by the shape of his muscular body...

t_ch: …你不敢说话，只能蹑手蹑脚地体认有关他的一切。爱上一个天才，让你觉得自己更像个笨蛋——直到有一天，哈尔博士突然宣誓了自己的爱情。
t_en: ...You dared not speak while you carefully took in every detail about him. Falling in love with a genius made you feel even dumber. Your feelings for him lasted until the day Dr. Howl announced the love of his life.

t_ch: 在那天，他宣布将终其一生追溯恩•德芮的过去、现在与将来。接着，他便彻底消失了踪迹，自此再无音讯。
t_en: On that day, he vowed to spend the rest of his life tracing the past, present, and future of Anne Duray. Then, he disappeared and has not been heard from since.

t_ch: 自那天起，你心中有关「美」的一切认知便尽数化作灰烬——烈焰自你脚下升起，顷刻间将你彻底吞没。
t_en: Since that day, everything you understand about "beauty" has turned to ashes — flames rise from below and devour you in an instant.

t_ch: 走开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 空气中悬浮着一丛气泡，七彩的光华流溢其中，恍惚间形成似有深意的画面。靠近时，一个像是黑塔的女声不带感情地低语：无法沟通。
t_en: Bubbles are floating in the air with a colorful glow gliding on their surfaces. For some reason, they form an image that seems to carry a profound meaning. As you approach them, a female voice that sounds similar to Herta's murmurs monotonously, "Unable to communicate."

t_ch: 触碰忆泡。
t_en: Touch the Memory Bubble.

t_ch: 你靠近忆泡，感到一丝凉意。凉意温柔地穿过你的皮肤、骨肉，穿过更深层的……
t_en: You approach the bubble and experience a chilly sensation that gently pierces through your skin, flesh, and bones, then goes down deeper...

t_ch: 你的视野中是一片粘稠的、起伏的、毫无美感可言的红泥——其中一些甚至在蠕动。你反应过来那是一群大头矮脚的红泥黏土人，凯尔萨斯星的真正统治者。
t_en: In your view is a stretch of sticky, unruly, and unattractive red mud — some part of it is even squirming. It occurs to you that it is a group of Claymen with big heads and short legs. They are the true rulers of the planet of Kalsas.

t_ch: 啊，你想，这儿好像是这帮泥巴人的御用议事厅！可这场会议与你何干？你忘了。不过你很清楚，只要出现在这里，你就有机会把它们肚子里的珍贵矿藏撬出来。
t_en: Oh, this place seems to be the Royal Council Chamber of the Claymen! You have forgotten why you are attending the meeting, but you know it is a golden opportunity to pry out the precious minerals from the bellies of the Claymen.

t_ch: 会议室里充斥着咕嘟咕嘟的闷响…就像是人淹没在沼泽中，被污泥封堵喉咙时绝望的呜咽——这是它们的语言，但你根本听不懂——因为你还没有植入联觉信标。
t_en: The meeting room reverberates with muffled gobbling noises... it sounds like the whines of despair made by a person drowning in a swamp as mud slowly blocks off their airways. This is the Claymen race's language, but you can't understand a word of it — you have yet to implant a Synesthesia Beacon.

t_ch: 这场会议真是无聊到令人绝望…「在凯尔萨斯星，人人都可以畅所欲言！」翻译官如是说。黏土女王满怀深情地望着你，而你正好奇它的眼睛究竟长在哪里。
t_en: The meeting is boring you to tears... "On Kalsas, anyone can speak their mind!" The interpreter relays to you. The Clay Queen gazes at you, and you wonder where the queen's eyes are.

t_ch: 「我们肩负使命——共同抵御凯尔萨斯星的灾难雨季！」联觉翻译官如是说。黏土国王慷慨激昂，而你正望向窗外，绞尽脑汁思考，泥巴羊究竟是怎样的口感。
t_en: The Synesthesia Interpreter says, "This mission rests upon our shoulders. Together, we will defend Kalsas from the calamity that is the wet season!" The Clay King is overcome with emotion. Meanwhile, you stare out the window, racking your brain as you ponder what kind of texture the meat of Claysheep would have.

t_ch: 「天啊，」你有点受不了了，「听人才激励部主管的口水话都比听这帮烂泥吐泡泡有意思。」你不禁翻了个白眼，却猛然发现国王正盯着你——
t_en: "By the stars!" You can't take it anymore. "Listening to the prattling of the Talent Motivation Department's top brass would be more interesting than this muck." You can't resist the urge to roll your eyes, but suddenly notice the king is staring directly at you.

t_ch: 还好，原来它只是想邀请你，这位来自公司的稀土管理顾问，上台演讲——可作为一个异乡人，你怎么可能懂什么「凯尔萨斯星的未来」呢？
t_en: Thankfully, the king just wants to invite you, a rare-earth management consultant from the IPC, to speak on the stage. Problem? You're just an outsider. What could you possibly know about the future of Kalsas?

t_ch: ——哈，有了！你那干瘪的眼球狡黠地、骨碌碌地转动，随后向国王致以标准且灿烂的微笑。随后，你走上讲台，口若悬河…当然，用的是你*想象中*的黏土语。
t_en: Haha. You've got it! You roll your dry eyeball slyly and swiftly, then flash the king a standard yet dazzling smile. You take to the stage and release an unending waterfall of sounds in what you IMAGINE their language to be.

t_ch: 一众泥巴听得声泪俱下…你真害怕它们会融化在自己的眼泪里。你大笑，对着翻译官摊手：「这颗星球已经没有希望了——因为黏土人根本活不过雨季！」
t_en: The crowd of Claymen cry as they hear you speak... you worry they might dissolve in a puddle of their own tears. You laugh and shrug toward the interpreter as you say, "This planet doesn't have a shred of hope, because the Claymen won't make it past the wet season!"

t_ch: 「他说凯尔萨斯星充满希望，」翻译官汗如雨下，「这是通用语…宇宙主义，你们懂的——加入公司，团结一致，沟通无障碍！」
t_en: "He says that there is so much hope for Kalsas," the interpreter says as he nervously sweats. "This is a common language... Universalism, you understand. By joining the IPC and being united, there will be no communication barriers!"

t_ch: 走开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 空气中悬浮着一丛气泡，七彩的光华流溢其中，恍惚间形成似有深意的画面。靠近时，一个像是黑塔的女声不带感情地低语：消化。
t_en: Bubbles are floating in the air with a colorful glow gliding on their surfaces. For some reason, they form an image that seems to carry a profound meaning. As you approach them, a female voice that sounds similar to Herta's murmurs monotonously, "Digestion."

t_ch: 触碰忆泡。
t_en: Touch the Memory Bubble.

t_ch: 你靠近忆泡，感到一丝凉意。凉意温柔地穿过你的皮肤、骨肉，穿过更深层的……
t_en: You approach the bubble and experience a chilly sensation that gently pierces through your skin, flesh, and bones, then goes down deeper...

t_ch: 你认为达尔薇博士多半是疯了。你瞥了瞥那佝偻、衰弱、干瘪的年迈身躯——她正透过那对厚到可怕的镜片无言地盯着你。
t_en: You're pretty sure Dr. Darvill has gone crazy. You glance at her hunched, frail, and shrunken husk of a body. She silently stares at you through her terrifyingly thick lenses.

t_ch: 这群*科学家*永远不懂得识时务。你现在只想把这沓写满了疯话的报告扔进壁炉里，然后赶紧打发她走人——老实说，你这辈子都不想再听到「生物」这个词了。
t_en: These SCIENTISTS never know how to read the air. All you want right now is to throw this report containing the ravings of a mad scientist into a furnace, then send her packing. You never want to hear the word "organism" again.

t_ch: 「这就是事实，」救命，那令人恼火的干枯嘴唇又开始颤动了，「我们生活在一只巨大的哺乳动物体内，而我们所处的位置大致是它的肠道……」
t_en: "This is fact." Someone, anyone, please help! Those dry infuriating lips are trembling again. "We live within the body of an enormous mammal, located most likely in its intestines..."

t_ch: 「够了！」你拍案而起——这女人简直是在羞辱你，羞辱整个丹尼索瓦公国！可她只是扶了扶眼镜，看起来完全不在乎你那几乎要涌出喉咙的剧烈怒火。
t_en: "I've had it!" You slam your hands on the desk as you stand up. This woman is clearly humiliating you and the entire Dukedom of Denisova! However, she merely adjusts her glasses, completely unfazed by the rage within you that is threatening to explode.

t_ch: 「殿下，您有考虑过如今酸雾为何越来越频繁吗？」她嘶哑，「我们必须全面禁止使用迪赛尔热机了…它排出的废烟会极大刺激它的肠道，导致更多肠液分泌……」
t_en: "Your Highness, have you ever considered why acid mists have become increasingly common recently?" She asks hoarsely. "We must completely ban the use of Diesel engines... the exhaust fumes these engines release are causing significant irritation of the creature's intestines, which leads to increased secretion of intestinal juices..."

t_ch: 「全面禁止」！你倒吸一口冷气。这女人说得倒轻巧。这会导致多么可怕的财政赤字啊！区区酸雾算什么？只要我们的工厂开足马力，拼命生产防护装备……
t_en: "Complete ban!?" You gasp. Her proposal is easier said than done. The fallout from such a ban would nose-dive the nation's finances into the red! What's the big deal with acid mist? As long as the factories continue ahead at full power and churn out more protective equipment...

t_ch: 哈，又是一笔丰厚的利润…你不禁为这想法击节叫好，露出了满意的笑容。但博士似乎误会了什么——她似乎将之视为你赞同她的提案，便又继续喋喋不休起来。
t_en: Ah, that gear would generate serious revenue... you can't help but feel pleased at this idea as a smile crosses your face. The doctor, however, seems to misread that smile. She seems to view it as you agreeing with her proposal, and starts to talk again.

t_ch: 「还有地震，」她清了清嗓子，「过去的一个月里，整个丹尼索瓦就已经震了三次。这意味着我们的宿主肠道活动变得频繁了——坏的那种……」
t_en: "There's also the earthquakes." She clears her throat. "In the past month, Denisova has had three seismic events. This means that there is increased activity within the host's intestines. Detrimental activity..."

t_ch: …到此为止吧。你实在是不想再听到她发表任何有关寄生虫和排泄物的隐喻了。可正当你意图下达逐客令时，却发觉大厅猛烈摇晃了起来，天花板也开始坍塌——
t_en: ...Time to end the conversation. You have no more interest in listening to her allusions to parasites and excrement. Just as you are about to order her to leave, you feel the hall sway violently. The ceiling begins to collapse.

t_ch: 「这就是我接下来要介绍的部分了，」她的声音依旧冷静，「我们预测到了第四次地震，一场规模空前的反刍——我们会被它的胃酸彻底溶解…无人可以幸免。」
t_en: "This was what I want to discuss next," she says with a voice that is as calm as ever. "We predicted there would be a fourth earthquake, a regurgitation at an unprecedented scale. We will be completely dissolved by its stomach acid... No one will survive."

t_ch: 走开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 空气中悬浮着一丛气泡，七彩的光华流溢其中，恍惚间形成似有深意的画面。靠近时，一个像是黑塔的女声不带感情地低语：大快朵颐。
t_en: Bubbles are floating in the air with a colorful glow gliding on their surfaces. For some reason, they form an image that seems to carry a profound meaning. As you approach them, a female voice that sounds similar to Herta's murmurs monotonously, "A feast."

t_ch: 触碰忆泡。
t_en: Touch the Memory Bubble.

t_ch: 你靠近忆泡，感到一丝凉意。凉意温柔地穿过你的皮肤、骨肉，穿过更深层的……
t_en: You approach the bubble and experience a chilly sensation that gently pierces through your skin, flesh, and bones, then goes down deeper...

t_ch: 你在许多电离气体聚集的星际云团中穿梭。长时间的旅行令你的消化循环腔感到不适——你真的好想用什么东西填满它…什么都可以！
t_en: You move through a nebula made of numerous ionized gases. Your long journey has made your digestive cavity feel upset. You desperately want to fill it with something... Anything!

t_ch: 会喷射闪亮气流的钢块、被关在铁笼子里的大火球、酥酥脆脆的岩屑盘…你吃过好多好多有趣的东西！但你已经吃腻了…你想吃*更好吃的*，吃*更多*！
t_en: You've devoured so many interesting items: A chunk of steel that shoots bright gas currents, a massive ball of fire trapped in a cage of iron, a crunchy rock debris disk... but you've grown tired of eating all that. You want is something TASTIER, and MORE of it!

t_ch: 你焦躁地甩动触手。吃不到东西令你感到不愉快…这片星云附近难道就没有好吃的东西吗？你有点烦闷，在高速粒子流中苦恼地转来转去。
t_en: You flail your tentacles restlessly. Not being able to eat makes you feel so hangry... There must be something tasty around the nebula, right? You feel a little annoyed as you circle about in a high-speed particle stream in misery.

t_ch: 突然，你的感觉球似乎捕捉到了什么：一种芬芳的、诱人的味道——那究竟是什么？会不会很好吃？你的胃丝开始兴奋地战栗——你已经等不及了……
t_en: All of a sudden, your sensory orb seems to pick up something — something fragrant, a scent that draws you in. What could it be? Is it something delicious? The filaments in your stomach tremble with excitement. You can't wait anymore...

t_ch: 你向那芳香的源头飞奔，无数腕管剧烈翕动——你已经很久没有过类似的感觉了…某种原始的冲动自外缘神经环贯入内胚层——这种如饥似渴的感觉！
t_en: You race off to the source of the aroma, countless tubes and feeders swinging about violently. You haven't had this kind of feeling in forever... a primitive impulse shoots into your endoderm from your peripheral nervous system. It's thirst and hunger!

t_ch: 那味道越来越剧烈，令你忍不住舒展整个伞盖，全身心地浸入这令你意乱神迷的馥郁香泽中…到了、就快到了！接下来就是大快朵颐的时间……
t_en: The scent grows more intense. You can't resist the urge to extend your umbrella of a body as you throw your whole being into this dazzling aroma... You are so close! So close! Now it is time for a grand feast...

t_ch: 映入视野的是一颗巨大的行星——它真的好大好大！年幼的你从未想过，行星竟然可以达到这种尺寸…你还以为它们都是可以直接一口吞掉的呢！
t_en: What comes into your field of vision is a massive planet. It is truly a big, big celestial body! You are still young, so you never imagined that a planet could be so big... You had previously assumed they were all small enough for you to swallow whole!

t_ch: 面前的这颗星星足足有你身体三倍大——这令你不禁感到好奇：自己的消化循环腔果真能够装下它吗？究竟要吃几天才能全部吃完？……
t_en: Yet the planet before you is easily three times the size of your body. This makes you wonder whether your digestive cavity could fit it. How many days would it take to consume the whole planet?

t_ch: 不过，你很快便放心下来。毕竟你有的是时间！你可是族群里最年幼的阿米巴黏菌，未来远远多于过去。你大可以想吃几天就吃几天——进食就是你的宿命！
t_en: Your worries slip away before long. After all, you have plenty of time! You are the youngest in your Amoeba Slime Mold tribe. You have far more days ahead of you than behind. You realize that you can eat for as many days as you like. Eating is your destiny!

t_ch: 想到这里，你欣快地钻入那氢与氦团聚而成的粘稠大气，开始寻找该从何处下口——要先尝尝炽热的熔融岩核吗？还是冰爽的氨云？你简直要挑花眼了……
t_en: With this in mind, you happily burrow into the viscous atmosphere made of hydrogen and helium. You start looking for a place to begin your meal. Do you want to taste the scorching molten core first? Or the cool clouds of ammonia? It's a buffet of planetary proportions...

t_ch: 走开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 空气中悬浮着一丛气泡，七彩的光华流溢其中，恍惚间形成似有深意的画面。靠近时，一个像是黑塔的女声不带感情地低语：可爱的宠物。
t_en: Bubbles are floating in the air with a colorful glow gliding on their surfaces. For some reason, they form an image that seems to carry a profound meaning. As you approach them, a female voice that sounds similar to Herta's murmurs monotonously, "Cute pet."

t_ch: 触碰忆泡。
t_en: Touch the Memory Bubble.

t_ch: 你靠近忆泡，感到一丝凉意。凉意温柔地穿过你的皮肤、骨肉，穿过更深层的……
t_en: You approach the bubble and experience a chilly sensation that gently pierces through your skin, flesh, and bones, then goes down deeper...

t_ch: 你感到空气中弥漫着一丝不安。他们说——主要是阿德，你们工程队里消息最灵通的那个小伙子说——皇帝已经向施工地派出了一支别动队，缘由暂不得而知。
t_en: You feel a sense of unease that pervades the air. You hear from the others — mostly from Ader, the one person on the team who's always getting the latest news — that the emperor has dispatched a special force to the construction site. The reason for this development remains unknown.

t_ch: 「听说他们已经在一号坞口登陆了。」阿德显得有点紧张，不停搓着他怀里的毛球。他管那生物叫「毛绒绒」。不得不说，这是个很土的名字。
t_en: "I heard they already arrived at Dock No. 1." Ader seems on edge as he incessantly rubs his hands against the fluff ball in his arms. He had named the creature "Fluffies." To be honest, it isn't the most creative name.

t_ch: 这毛球在你们的施工队中广受喜爱，以至于你们几乎人手一只。你当然也有——你管他叫「大橘」——只因为它是橘色的。此刻，它正在你的肩头呼呼大睡呢。
t_en: These fluff balls are beloved among the construction team, so much so that practically every member has one. Of course, you do, too. You named yours "Big O" because it's orange. Your pet is currently snoring away on your shoulder.

t_ch: 你怜爱地揉了揉大概是它头顶的部分。这稍微令你感到宽慰，就像平时那样…如果没有它，你早就受不了这严酷的施工环境和一成不变的工作生活了。
t_en: You lovingly rub what you assume is most likely its head. Like always, this brings you a bit of comfort. If you didn't have this little pet, you wouldn't be able to stand the harsh construction environment and the monotony of work and life.

t_ch: 「我听说啊，只是听说，」你感觉阿德快要把毛绒绒的毛发薅光了，「皇帝应该是知道了毛球的事…那别动队八成就是专门来消灭它们的。」
t_en: "So, I've heard something. Keep in mind I don't know for sure," Ader starts to say. You think he is well on his way to pulling out all of the Fluffie's fur. "The emperor probably knows about the Fluffies... It's quite likely the special force is here to exterminate them."

t_ch: 你感到不解。看看大橘——多可爱啊！难道有谁能抗拒抚摸那柔软的绒毛，听它发出满意的咕噜声吗？退一步讲，它们明明温顺得很…到底为什么要消灭它们？
t_en: You can't even fathom it. Look how cute Big O is! Can anyone resist petting its delightfully soft fur or listening to its satisfied purring? If you think about it, they are clearly docile creatures... Why exterminate them?

t_ch: 「嘁！」你的工头将脑袋埋进属于他自己的毛球里猛吸了一口，「那个老皇帝…打发我们来这鬼地方修坟墓就算了，连宠物都不让养？！真该死……」
t_en: "Ahhh!" Your supervisor buries his head in his fluff ball and takes in a gulp of air. "That decrepit old emperor... Firstly he sends us to make his tomb at this godforsaken place, and now we can't even have pets!? Ridiculous..."

t_ch: 昏暗的宿舍里陷入短暂的沉默。你看了看两人，又望向窗外，看到悬在天空中的那颗机械眼球依旧发散令人压抑的微光。你们不约而同地叹气。
t_en: The dim dormitory falls silent for a moment. You look at your two coworkers, then look out the window. You see the mechanical eye that remains suspended in the sky, creating a repressive atmosphere with its feeble light. You all sigh together as if on cue.

t_ch: 大橘在你肩头扭了扭。它好像醒了——大约是因为宿舍门外传来急促的脚步声。「他们来了…！」阿德催促你们，喉头似乎在震颤：「快！把它们藏起来——」
t_en: Big O stirs on your shoulder. It seems to have woken up, likely because of the hurried footsteps outside the door of the dormitory. "They're here!" Ader urges you all to move fast. His throat seems to tremble as he speaks, "Hurry! Hide them..."

t_ch: 催促的话语戛然而止。正当你心生疑惑，想要回头确认他到底发生了什么事时…却只在黑暗中隐约发觉一张缀满毛发的血盆大口。
t_en: Ader's words are abruptly cut off. Just as you start to worry and want to turn around to see what happened to him... you faintly make out a giant hairy mouth dripping with blood in the darkness.

t_ch: 走开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 空气中悬浮着一丛气泡，七彩的光华流溢其中，恍惚间形成似有深意的画面。靠近时，一个像是黑塔的女声不带感情地低语：钙质化。
t_en: Bubbles are floating in the air with a colorful glow gliding on their surfaces. For some reason, they form an image that seems to carry a profound meaning. As you approach them, a female voice that sounds similar to Herta's murmurs monotonously, "Calcification."

t_ch: 触碰忆泡。
t_en: Touch the Memory Bubble.

t_ch: 你靠近忆泡，感到一丝凉意。凉意温柔地穿过你的皮肤、骨肉，穿过更深层的……
t_en: You approach the bubble and experience a chilly sensation that gently pierces through your skin, flesh, and bones, then goes down deeper...

t_ch: 「他们怎么说？」你百无聊赖，把玩着手中的黏质定向炸药，「那帮老古板还是不让我们开炮？」你将炸药保险销拔下，又在炸裂读秒结束前将其插回。
t_en: "What did they say?" You feel bored to death. You fiddle with the adhesive explosive charge in your hand. "Those old geezers still aren't going to give us the green light to open fire?" You pull out the pin on the explosive, only putting it back in a few seconds before the countdown ends.

t_ch: 站在你身旁的菜鸟似乎还没能习惯你这极其危险的爱好。她显得有些惶恐：「是的，教授。他们害怕爆炸产生的高热使病毒活化……」她咽了咽口水。
t_en: The newbie standing next to you seems like she isn't used to your dangerous antics. She appears to be somewhat terrified. "That's right, professor. They worry that the high temperatures produced by the explosion will activate the virus..." She gulps.

t_ch: 你瘪瘪嘴。「那他们喊我们来做什么？」你本想痛骂他们一顿，却一时不知该说什么好。「一群*怂包*…」半晌，你悻悻地拿起手边的报告读了起来。
t_en: You pout. "Then why did they get us to come here?" You originally wanted to scold them, but now are at a loss for words. "These SCAREDY CATS..." After a while, you angrily pick up the report next to you and read it.

t_ch: 「细胞癌化，软组织钙化…」你本以为那帮老古董都是一群狗嘴里吐不出象牙的货色，可这份报告确实有点意思…可惜，只是读报告并不能安抚你躁动的心。
t_en: "Cells become cancerous, and soft tissues calcify..." You initially had assumed these old fogies were only capable of churning out useless garbage, but this report is actually interesting... Unfortunately, reading reports cannot calm your anxious heart.

t_ch: 你叹气，昂起头，眺望着远方*惨白*的遗迹。经过你们测定，这片宛如骨刺的扭曲废墟已有整整四百个琥珀纪的历史——
t_en: You sigh and lift your head. You look at the PALLID ruins in the distance. Your analysis revealed that these twisted ruins, which look like bone spurs, have a history that goes back four hundred Amber Eras.

t_ch: 尽管如此，它却不知因何故保持着四百纪之前的崭新面貌。这一谜题实在令你抓心挠肝…你可是资深的武装考古学派专家！解开一切历史谜题就是你的志愿！
t_en: Despite its age, it mysteriously appears as if brand-new, just as how it would have looked four hundred AEs ago. You are confounded by this enigma... You are a seasoned Armed Archaeologist! You long to unravel all historical mysteries!

t_ch: 可他们竟然不许你践行学派传统，在那骨刺表面轰开大洞，然后带着价值连城的文物扬长而去…你感到恼火，但还是选择妥协——毕竟他们的担忧不无道理。
t_en: Yet they are holding you back from using your school's tradition. You should be blowing a hole into the surface of this bone spur and carrying off valuable artifacts. The fact that they won't permit it makes you seethe with anger, but you choose to compromise. You recognize that their concerns are not groundless.

t_ch: 可不能把命赔进去，你想，还是走老路子吧——要是有命挣钱没命花，那才是蠢材呢。你咂咂嘴，开始考虑下一步该如何行动……
t_en: You can't pay for this with your life. You settle for doing things the old way. You work to live, not live to work. You smack your lips in anticipation and start to plan your next steps...

t_ch: …这思考没能持续太久。一道嘹亮的嗓音打破了你的思绪：「老大，我们找着好东西了！」你认得，那是劳恩…可他在说什么？你明明告诉他们原地待命……
t_en: ...You don't get to think for long. A loud voice breaks your train of thought. "Hey boss, we found something good!" It's Rowen... but what is he saying? You've clearly told him to sit still and wait for further instructions.

t_ch: 全完了！——你猛然意识到这一点。你想喝止他，却发现自己发不出声…毫无疑问，你正在由内自外快速*硬化*——就像眼前那几尊熟悉的骇人骨雕……
t_en: Oh no! You suddenly realize something. You want to stop him, only to discover that you can't speak... Beyond the shadow of a doubt, you are experiencing rapid PETRIFACTION from inside out, just like those familiar-looking yet terrifying bone sculptures in front of you...

t_ch: 走开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 空气中悬浮着一丛气泡，七彩的光华流溢其中，恍惚间形成似有深意的画面。靠近时，一个像是黑塔的女声不带感情地低语：闲暇时光。
t_en: Bubbles are floating in the air with a colorful glow gliding on their surfaces. For some reason, they form an image that seems to carry a profound meaning. As you approach them, a female voice that sounds similar to Herta's murmurs monotonously, "Free Time."

t_ch: 触碰忆泡。
t_en: Touch the Memory Bubble.

t_ch: 你靠近忆泡，感到一丝凉意。凉意温柔地穿过你的皮肤、骨肉，穿过更深层的……
t_en: You approach the bubble and experience a chilly sensation that gently pierces through your skin, flesh, and bones, then goes down deeper...

t_ch: 你睡眼惺忪。房间被遮光系数高达<unbreak>99%</unbreak>的复合材料窗帘遮得严严实实——若不借助心灵感应触须，你仅凭肉眼很难分辨空间的构造…尽管你自五十年前就住在这里。
t_en: Your eyes are bleary from sleep. The composite material of the bedroom's curtains effectively blocks out <unbreak>99%</unbreak> of light. Were it not for your psychic tentacles, you would be hard-pressed to discern the structure of the space surrounding you with your eyes alone, even though you've been living here since fifty years ago.

t_ch: 正好，窗外似乎也是一派沉闷——粘稠的阴云在低空中聚合、翻滚——气温不高，你想，入秋了。这天气正适合睡觉…怪不得你睡得这么香。
t_en: It seems like the weather outside is quite gloomy. Thick, dark clouds hang low in the sky as they gather and roil. The temperature is low, and you realize it's already autumn. This kind of weather is perfect for sleeping. No wonder you slept so well.

t_ch: 你离开温暖的被窝。掀开窗帘，景致果然与你感应一致。看来你还没有老。你又转身，向那台老电视投出一道灵能波。电视应声启动——现在正播放午间天气预报。
t_en: You leave your warm blankets and pull open the curtains, greeted by a landscape that matches what you've sensed. Looks like you are not old yet. You turn toward the aged TV and direct a psychic energy beam toward it. The TV lights up in response. The midday weather forecast is on.

t_ch: 「中午十二时起，」电视上的迭瓦戎女性声音很是甜蜜，「孔泰主城区大部将有持续性重金属降雪，建议各位市民尽可能停止户外活动……」
t_en: "Starting at noon," the Dyvaron female on the TV has a sweet voice, "forecasts show that heavy metal snowfall will continue throughout most of the Comte metropolitan area. Residents are advised to avoid outdoor activities as much as possible..."

t_ch: 你昨天才在学校学了这个——孔泰星地表温度极高，重金属化合物在高温影响下蒸发、上升至对流层，冷凝，与大气中的无机酸结合，再以降水的形式返回地表……
t_en: Just yesterday, you learned at school that the surface temperature of the planet Comte is extremely high. Heavy metal compounds undergo evaporation due to the high temperatures, which causes them to rise to the troposphere. They then condensate and combine with the inorganic acids in the atmosphere, after which they return to the surface in the form of precipitation...

t_ch: …在心中默默复习了这个小小的知识点后，你感到十分满意。智慧生物就是喜欢为自己拥有他人不曾拥有的冷僻知识感到骄傲。你嘴角上扬，转身走向厨房。
t_en: ...After quietly reviewing this bit of new-found knowledge in your mind, you swell with satisfaction. Intelligent lifeforms always enjoy taking pride in knowing obscure trivia that others don't. The corners of your mouth rise up as you turn to go to the kitchen.

t_ch: 你为自己冲调了一杯热可可。它就是这天气的主角——还有什么比下金属雪时捧一杯热可可，团在被窝里，看着钟意的流媒体视频剧集更惬意的呢？
t_en: You make yourself a cup of hot cocoa, the drink of choice for this kind of weather. What could possibly be better than enjoying hot cocoa while snuggled up in a nice thick blanket and streaming a drama you enjoy as heavy metal snows down?

t_ch: 就这么做吧，你对自己说。杯中的褐色液体冒着热气，你的心灵感应触须慵懒地趴在脑门上。然后是胃——甜美的口感与舒适的温度顷刻间充盈了你的身体……
t_en: That's exactly what you'll do, you say to yourself. The brown liquid in your mug is piping hot. Your psychic tentacles lazily flop down on your head. A sweet flavor and comfy warmth flood your body as the cocoa enters your stomach...

t_ch: 你感到舒展，随后又将灵能波投向你的台式电脑。《流星怪盗卢布朗》《绝命化妆师》《弗莱特博士》…看起来都不错。赞美星际和平影视！
t_en: You feel relaxed and direct a psychic energy beam toward your desktop computer. All these shows look pretty good — Leblanc: The Meteor Thief, Breaking Good, Dr. Flat... Thank the stars for Interastral Peace Media!

t_ch: 你就这样团坐在自适应椅上，盖着绒毯，手捧温热的马克杯…你的闲暇时光正在进行中。窗外的雪片自天空悠悠落下，散发荧光，随风飘荡——但那已经与你无关了。
t_en: You curl up on an adaptive chair and cover yourself with a fleecy blanket. There's a nice warm cup of hot cocoa in your hands... Your free time has truly begun. Outside the window, snowflakes gently fall from the sky. They give off a florescent glow as they drift wherever the wind takes them — but that no longer has anything to do with you.

t_ch: 走开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 豹~
t_en: Seal~

t_ch: 我也是豹，我是迷糊姐姐的圣兽…难道我看起来不像吗？
t_en: I am a seal too. I am Miss Daze's divine beast... Don't I look like a seal?

t_ch: (;ﾟдﾟ)
t_en: (;ﾟдﾟ)

t_ch: 我、我只是穿上了黄金款皮肤而已，这是我抽奖抽出来的——我可不是在晒嗡！
t_en: I... just put on a golden outfit. I got this from a reward raffle. I... I'm not showboating!

t_ch: 嘿，有胆来比一比吗？
t_en: Hey, you've got enough guts to compete with me?

t_ch: 哼哼，追得上吗？
t_en: Heh, can you even catch up?

t_ch: 吃我的鳞粉吧~
t_en: Eat my wing powder~!

t_ch: 好厉害的人，我不是对手……
t_en: You're so awesome. I definitely can't keep up...

t_ch: 你该不会是要吃我吧？别，我、我死啦~
t_en: Y—You're not going to eat me, are you? D—Don't eat me! I—I'm dead waaah~

t_ch: 我成功了！
t_en: I did it!

t_ch: ( Φ ω Φ )
t_en: ( Φ ω Φ )

t_ch: 我的穿墙术终于练成了嗡，现在我也是{RUBY_B#雅努斯}「双开门」{RUBY_E#}的祭司了嗡~
t_en: I finally learned how to phase through walls! Now, I too am a priest of the {RUBY_B#Janus}"twin doors"{RUBY_E#}!

t_ch: 你为什么能嵌进里面？
t_en: How are you embedded in there?

t_ch: 嘿嘿，这就是穿墙术！不过现在的位置还不完美，等我挪动一下~
t_en: Hehe, this is the wall-phasing technique. But my current position isn't great. Let me move a bit.

t_ch: 你只有翅膀露出来了。
t_en: I can only see your wings stick out.

t_ch: 我、我才刚开始学！等我挪动一下……
t_en: I—I just started learning this technique! Let me move around a bit...

t_ch: 呼~好了，这下就是完整的穿墙了嗡。
t_en: Phew~ Alright, we're through the wall now.

t_ch: 偷偷告诉你一个秘密：「双开门」其实不止两扇，而是无穷扇！不过我只见过三扇……
t_en: Let me tell you a secret: The "twin doors" aren't just two doors, but endless doors! I've only seen three so far though...

t_ch: 蝴蝶纹丝不动，仿佛已经和这东西融为了一体……
t_en: The butterfly doesn't move at all, as though completely merged together with this thing...

t_ch: 我来拽你！
t_en: Let me pull you!

t_ch: 别、别拽我！我才刚开始学，等我挪动一下……
t_en: D—Don't pull me! I'm only just learning what to do. Let me shift a bit...

t_ch: 快来吃我~
t_en: Come and eat me~

t_ch: 你是来吃土的吗？不如来吃蝶吧嗡！
t_en: Are you here to eat dirt? Why not try a bit of butterfly?

t_ch: 泥土味苦，但我甜——多吃甜食有利健康嗡~
t_en: Mud tastes bitter, but I am sweet... Eating sweet things is good for your health~

t_ch: 英雄们，从这里往前的路会很危险嗡。即便如此，你们依旧要启程吗？
t_en: Heroes, the road ahead is going to be dangerous~ Are you sure you still want to go?

t_ch: 也好，英雄总是要去书写传奇篇章的…记得回家看看啊嗡。
t_en: Okay. Heroes always have to inscribe legendary chapters... Remember to come home and visit.

t_ch: 这些大高个子穿得都好土啊，一点也不符合我家太太的审美……
t_en: These big guys are all dressed so unfashionably, nothing like the style my lady likes...

t_ch: …幸好有我做点缀嗡~
t_en: ...But luckily I'm here to do a little embroidery~

t_ch: 嗡~这么长时间以来，我一直在靠自己的翅膀飞翔。没想到在水里，不用费力拍打翅膀也能也能「飞」起来。
t_en: All this time I've relied on these wings of mine to fly. Who would've thought in the water, I can "fly" just fine without having to flap my wings.

t_ch: 我的朋友啊，成为水吧嗡~
t_en: Be water, my friend~

t_ch: 这金黄色的液体真是香甜可口，我要去和更多同伴分享嗡。
t_en: This golden nectar is so sweet. I want to share it with all my friends.

t_ch: 偷黄金血的贼！
t_en: Thief! Someone's stealing golden blood!

t_ch: 偷？这怎么能叫偷呢嗡，明明我也是金黄色的——应该是志趣相投才对嗡！
t_en: Stealing? How do you figure that? Why, I'm golden myself, too — and as they say, like fancies like.

t_ch: 不对，怎么感觉晕晕的，难道是要长脑子了嗡？
t_en: No, why am I feeling so dizzy right now? Don't tell me it's my brain growing.

t_ch: 喝多了会出问题。
t_en: Drinking too much always causes problems.

t_ch: 出问题？怎么会…好像是有点神志不清了嗡。
t_en: Problems? But how... I'm starting to feel a little delirious.

t_ch: 给我也来一口。
t_en: Can I have a sip?

t_ch: 你也想喝嗡？那可不行，这坛都是属于我一只蝶…奇怪，头突然好晕啊嗡。
t_en: Wanna try some? No way, this jar's all mine... That's weird, I suddenly feel dizzy.

t_ch: 奇怪，为什么那帮大个子就不怕烫呢……
t_en: That's strange. How come those big guys aren't worried about getting burned...

t_ch: 果然我的耐热修炼还不够，得再加把劲儿才行嗡！
t_en: Seems like I haven't done enough heat resistance training. I need to go all out!

t_ch: 万物本是一，一生二，二生三；太阳为父，月亮为母；父母在，不远游……
t_en: All things are one, from which comes two, which splits into three. The sun is the father; the moon the mother: Wander not from filial duty...

t_ch: 如此便可远离黑暗蒙昧，嗡~
t_en: So one avoids darkness and folly~

t_ch: 唉，我也知道自己不可能进入壁画…但我好想和传说中的英雄们一起冒险啊嗡！
t_en: *sigh* I know I can't go into the mural... But I really want to go on a heroic adventure with heroes of legend!

t_ch: 记得在那个时代，死亡曾经比现在更柔软、更温暖……
t_en: I remember that, back in those days, death was a lot softer and warmer than it is now...

t_ch: 看吧嗡，黄金色的池水自高空流下，其中蕴藏着多少权与力？雨水是公平的，下给富人也下给穷人，但池水不是……
t_en: Look! *flutter* The golden pool waters are pouring down from the skies. How much power and might is contained therein? Rain water is equal opportunity — it falls on both the rich and the poor. But not this pool of water...

t_ch: 终有一天，我也要沐浴在这黄金色的池水中，受万人仰视。这才叫不负蝶生啊嗡~
t_en: One of these days I'm going to bathe in those golden waters, with thousands of people staring up at me. That's what it really means to have value in my life.

t_ch: 是没见过的勇士呢…长相倒是蛮可爱的，我喜欢嗡…
t_en: Now there's a warrior I've never seen before... Pretty cute, too... I like it...

t_ch: 宝宝你是一只会说话的蝴蝶…
t_en: Babe, you are a talking butterfly...

t_ch: 嗡是什么意思？
t_en: What do you mean?

t_ch: 我一眼就看出你不是人！
t_en: It's obvious that you're not a real human!

t_ch: 欸~你们竟然能识破我的伪装，难道你们也是我家太太的祭司？好久没见到它了嗡…
t_en: Wow~ You actually saw straight through my disguise. Don't tell me you're my lady's priest, are you? I haven't seen them in a long time...

t_ch: 可惜，如果你们也是若虫的话，我们就能在一起愉快玩耍了嗡。
t_en: What a shame! If you were also nymphs, we could have had a lot of fun together.

t_ch: 这个讲师，压根不懂黄金时代的历史，瞎讲一通嗡……
t_en: This lecturer now has no idea about the history of The Golden Age. He's just talking a load of baloney...

t_ch: 你问我懂不懂？我当然懂嗡！
t_en: Do I understand it all? Of course I do!

t_ch: 嗡嗡——太好听了吧！仿佛在听我家太太歌唱一样嗡~
t_en: That sounds amazing! It's like I'm listening to my lady singing~

t_ch: 据说这种乐器受到了迷糊姐姐的赐福…没想到能弹奏出如此清晰的旋律嗡~
t_en: Apparently, this instrument was blessed by the Miss Daze... I never expected it to sound so crisp~

t_ch: 这位灰发的听众，请问你有何评价嗡？
t_en: Dear gray-haired listener: How do you think it sounds?

t_ch: 很不错。
t_en: Fantastic.

t_ch: 是吧！好似清泉涌流，让人和虫听了都身心愉悦嗡！
t_en: Isn't it! It's like a torrent, bubbling up to bring joy to people and insects!

t_ch: 我上我也行。
t_en: At this rate, I can go play it too.

t_ch: 没想到你居然也会演奏乐器，改天一定要让我听听嗡！
t_en: I never knew you played an instrument. You have to let me hear you play one day!

t_ch: 不好不坏。
t_en: Just okay.

t_ch: 想必你一定听过更加优美的乐曲，也推荐给我吧嗡！
t_en: I'm sure you must have heard more beautiful music played before. Please, give me some recommendations!

t_ch: 这地方光秃秃的，简直一点也不美观嗡…所有柱子上都该有一盏灯才对嗡！
t_en: It's basically barren in here, nothing to write home about... They don't even have lamps for these pillars!

t_ch: 为了拯救房间的布局，我不介意亲自做灯——我要追求极致嗡！
t_en: I wouldn't mind hanging lamps up myself if it saved the design of this room — I guess I'm a perfectionist!

t_ch: Zzz…嗯，我睡着了？
t_en: Zzz... Eh? Sorry, I dozed off for a sec there.

t_ch: 我、我不知道这是有人的房间——我这就离开嗡！
t_en: I—I didn't know this room was occupied — I'll be off right away then!

t_ch: 这里的蒸汽可真强劲，像屏障一样，我根本没法触及水面嗡……
t_en: The steam here is so strong, it's like a barrier. I can't even touch any of the water...

t_ch: 但我不会放弃泡澡的尝试——这就是我，逆风飞翔的蝴蝶！
t_en: But I'm still not giving up on my chance to take a bath — here I am, a tiny butterfly powering into the wind!

t_ch: 唉，这些植物感觉都没什么精神嗡，和以前比还是差远了……
t_en: *sigh* These plants feel as though they're completely lifeless. Now it's even worse than before...

t_ch: 真怀念我们的大大…也不知它现在过得怎样嗡。
t_en: I really miss our boss... Hope everything is going well for them.

t_ch: 吼吼，没有被那面镜子分散注意，反而朝着我走过来了吗？
t_en: Ho ho, you're coming straight this way. I see the mirror didn't distract you, did it?

t_ch: 看来你有成为侦探的潜力，和我一起揭开这个世界的真相吧嗡。
t_en: Looks as though you've got what it takes to potentially become a detective, then. Come with me, and we'll expose the truth behind this world.

t_ch: 谢谢你陌生人！但是蝴蝶藏在对面的篮子里。
t_en: Thank you, stranger! But the butterfly is in the opposite basket.

t_ch: …要启程了吗？
t_en: ...Are you setting off on a journey?

t_ch: 来点亮色~
t_en: How about a splash of color~

t_ch: 从未有过的感觉…
t_en: A feeling you've never had before...

t_ch: 干了嗷兄弟们！
t_en: Down in one, brothers!

t_ch: 烫烫烫！
t_en: Hot, hot, hot!

t_ch: 膜拜我吧~
t_en: Worship me~

t_ch: 并非专家！
t_en: I am no expert!

t_ch: 安可！
t_en: Encore!

t_ch: 这样才叫完美嘛~
t_en: Now this is perfect, don't you think~

t_ch: Zzz…
t_en: Zzz...

t_ch: 我是叛逆的蝶~
t_en: I am a rebelfly~

t_ch: 在哪里呢…
t_en: Where is it...

t_ch: 阿格莱雅为若虫打造的聚居地。分散各地的眷属们在此合宿，必要时也能出来遛弯。
t_en: A haven created by Aglaea for the nymphs. The scattered Titankin reside here, coming out for a stroll when need be.

t_ch: 这些若虫原本亲密无间，却出于某种原因无法相见。礼赞命运，让我们再次看到了这幅场面。
t_en: These nymphs were once tight-knit as iron chains, but could not see one another for reasons unknown. Blessed is destiny, that we are able to witness their union once more.

t_ch: 这两只若虫原本亲密无间，却出于某种原因无法相见。礼赞命运，让我们再次看到了这幅场面。
t_en: These two nymphs were once tight-knit as iron chains, but could not see one another for reasons unknown. Blessed is destiny, that we are able to witness their union once more.

t_ch: 喀喀~
t_en: Crrrk...

t_ch: 喀喀喀！
t_en: Crrkrrk!

t_ch: 喀喀喀喀喀喀喀喀！
t_en: Crrrrrkkkk!

t_ch: 家人，别卷了…
t_en: Please stop that, my brethren...

t_ch: 这里似乎没有更多若虫了…去告诉衣匠吧。
t_en: There are seemingly no other nymphs here... Let's tell the Garmentmaker.

t_ch: 已经把褔坛调整到正确的状态了…去看看墙上那些若虫有没有变化吧。
t_en: The altar has been shifted to the correct position... Let's see if anything changed with those nymphs on the wall.

t_ch: 这些若虫的排列…
t_en: The way these nymphs are lined up...

t_ch: 似乎是某种暗示？
t_en: It looks like there's some kind of hidden message?

t_ch: 预备…起！
t_en: Get ready... go!

t_ch: 我感觉到了…
t_en: I feel it...

t_ch: 又是熟悉的感觉…
t_en: It's the familiar feeling again...

t_ch: 好像要长脑子了…
t_en: It feels like my head is getting smarter...

t_ch: 多谢你，贵客…我注意到你已经获取了预言所指的目标。真是神奇，你身上果然还藏着我们想不到的秘密。
t_en: Thank you so much, esteemed guest... I notice that you have already obtained the target mentioned in the prophecy. Remarkable. You truly bear secrets we could never imagine.

t_ch: 这些织者日后会成为圣城重要的助力。我为你准备了一点薄礼，还请收下。
t_en: The weavers will be an invaluable help to the holy city. I have prepared you a small token of my gratitude. Please, take it.

t_ch: 欧洛尼斯的一丝力量已经为这只虫所有——以脚垫的形式。
t_en: A sliver of the power of Oronyx has already been bestowed upon this insect... in the form of a foot pad.

t_ch: 奇怪...这上面为什么没有图案？
t_en: Strange... Why isn't there a pattern?

t_ch: 总感觉缺了什么线索...难道和那些创生若虫有关?
t_en: Feels like we're missing some clues... Could have it something to do with those Creation Nymphs?

t_ch: 多谢你，贵客，我注意到你已经获取了预言所指的目标。
t_en: Thank you so much, esteemed guest... I notice that you have already obtained the target mentioned in the prophecy.

t_ch: 其他地方应该还有怀揣赐福的若虫，贵客可以去别的衣匠处看看。
t_en: There must be other blessed nymphs in other areas. Esteemed guest, try taking a look around other Garmentmakers.

t_ch: 这里好漂亮喀…
t_en: It's beautiful here...

t_ch: 吾主会找到我吗…
t_en: Can my master locate me...

t_ch: 为我找些对手来喀！
t_en: Find some opponents for me!

t_ch: 完美的平衡喀。
t_en: Perfect balance.

t_ch: 若虫们的数量越来越多了。或许终有一天，这里会被虫鸣声填满。
t_en: The number of nymphs is growing. Maybe one day, this place will be filled with melodious chirping.

t_ch: 一个有些吓人的猜测从你脑海中浮现：有没有可能，这些若虫只在被你观测到时才会出现在这里。
t_en: A frightening speculation floats to the front of your mind: Is there a possibility, that these nymphs only appear when they are observed by you?

t_ch: 听说阿格莱雅会驱使若虫为自己服务…它们平时都住在这里吗？
t_en: There's a rumor that Aglaea commands these nymphs to serve her... Do they live here all the time?

t_ch: 一道沉默又威严的铁艺栅栏门横亘在此处。
t_en: A quiet and foreboding wrought iron fence blocks the way.

t_ch: 喔，不对，用「沉默」来形容它恐怕不大合适。因为，你似乎听到了一阵若有若无的沉吟——
t_en: Mm, "quiet" isn't the right word to describe it, because at this moment, you seem to hear faint muttering...

t_ch: 「不要靠近，不要靠近…」铁艺栅栏门如是说。
t_en: "Venture no closer, no closer..." the iron gate seems to say.

t_ch: 当然，这声音也有可能来自门后的那位惴惴不安的先生——你倒是不妨问问他：他到底为什么要特地在这里为一道冰冷的栅栏门配音。
t_en: Then again, the voice could have come from the nervous man behind the gate. You approach him to ask why on earth he is playing ventriloquist for the gate.

t_ch: 不要靠近…不要靠近我……
t_en: Venture no closer... to me...

t_ch: 请问是你在自言自语吗？
t_en: Might I ask why you're talking to yourself?

t_ch: 贝洛伯格的铁门居然会说话？
t_en: Belobog's iron gate can talk?

t_ch: …啊！您是在问我吗？
t_en: ...agh! Are you asking me?

t_ch: 我实在是太紧张了，没能注意到您…万分抱歉！
t_en: I was simply too nervous to have noticed you... I'm truly sorry!

t_ch: 你在紧张什么？
t_en: What're you nervous about?

t_ch: 难道你是逃犯？
t_en: Are you on the lam?

t_ch: 不不不，这怎么可能！我、我向来遵纪守法……
t_en: Nonono, of course not! I, I've always been a law-abiding citizen...

t_ch: 我长得有那么好看吗？
t_en: Am I that attractive then?

t_ch: 哈！您、您可真幽默……
t_en: Ha! You, you're a funny one...

t_ch: 是，后、「后巷」……
t_en: It's, the ba— "back alley"...

t_ch: 您…没听说吗？最近有些不、不干净的东西在这「后巷」里作乱……
t_en: Have you... not heard? So-Some ghostly goings-on have been happening in the "back alley" recently...

t_ch: 「后巷」？
t_en: The "back alley"?

t_ch: 啊…您、您难道不是本地人吗？
t_en: Ah... are, are you not a resident of this neighborhood?

t_ch: 「后巷」就是这剧院背后的小巷…是我们这些穷人住的地方哪。
t_en: The "back alley" is an alleyway behind the theater... It's where we poor people live.

t_ch: 不干净的东西？
t_en: Ghostly goings-on?

t_ch: 具体的…我、我也不是很清楚……
t_en: I, I'm not too sure about the details...

t_ch: 我只知道它、它们就混在我们中间…而且，已经有好几个邻居遭殃了！
t_en: I just know that th—they're among us... and they've gotten their hands on some of the residents!

t_ch: 对我不必尊称「您」…
t_en: You don't have to be this polite with me...

t_ch: 喔、喔！谢谢……
t_en: Oh, ooh! Thank goodness...

t_ch: 为什么不离开那里呢？
t_en: Why don't you leave the place then?

t_ch: 哎…都是因为这道栅栏门！
t_en: *sigh* ...it's all because of this gate!

t_ch: 我和邻居们都想离开这儿啊…可怎么也没办法打开这大门，只好定时来这儿透透气……
t_en: The neighbors and I wish to leave this place too... but we can't seem to get it open at all. All we can do is come out occasionally for some fresh air...

t_ch: …啊。时间似乎不早了，我得回去了。
t_en: ...Ah. It's getting late, and I have to return.

t_ch: 哎…真是倒霉，倒霉！该死的「后巷」！
t_en: Argh... just my luck! This blasted "back alley"!

t_ch: 支支吾吾地撂下几句让人摸不着头脑的话后，面前的男人一边连连摇头，一边颤抖地消失在剧院后的阴影下。
t_en: The man up ahead mutters some confusing words as his head shakes incessantly, before disappearing into the shadows of the theater.

t_ch: （真是奇怪的男人…不知道以后还会不会在这里遇到他。）
t_en: (What a strange man... I wonder if I'll run into him again.)

t_ch: 熟悉的铁艺栅栏门和熟悉的男人。眼前的光景恰如彼时——只是男人眉间的皱纹愈发深重了。
t_en: The familiar wrought iron gate, and a familiar man — the scene felt almost identical to the other time, if not for the deepened creases across the man's brow.

t_ch: 该死，那玩意到底是打哪儿来的……
t_en: Bah, where on earth did those things come from...

t_ch: 发生什么事了？
t_en: What happened?

t_ch: 又是不干净的东西？
t_en: The ghostly goings-on again?
...Who's there!?

t_ch: 哈，原来是您啊。还记得我不？先前和您打过照面的……
t_en: Oh, hello, dear stranger. Do you remember me? We've met before...

t_ch: 哎…能看见熟面孔真是太好了——我已经有点受不了了……
t_en: Oh, it's wonderful to see a familiar face. I was just about at my wit's ends...

t_ch: 怎么受不了了？
t_en: Why is that?

t_ch: 其他熟人呢？
t_en: Where are the others?

t_ch: 我、我的那些个朋友啊、邻居啊…都莫名其妙消失了！
t_en: Th—Those friends and neighbors of mine... they've mysteriously disappeared!

t_ch: 已经很久没见到他们了…这多半是「那些东西」捣的鬼！
t_en: I haven't seen them for a long time... no thanks to those "things"!

t_ch: 我听说，它们把人抓到后就、就会……
t_en: I hear, that after catching innocent people, th—they will...

t_ch: 之后…它们…会变成我们的样子！然后再去骗那些倒霉的家伙…！
t_en: And then... they'll... turn into us and then go search out for more unwitting innocents...!

t_ch: 眼睛、鼻子、嘴巴…全都一个模样，分毫不差……
t_en: Eyes, nose, mouth... they all look the same, not a speck of difference...

t_ch: 不要相信它们…您可千万要留个心眼，不要相信它们……
t_en: Don't trust them... you must watch out for yourself. Don't trust them...

t_ch: 男人变得近乎歇斯底里，胡言乱语地退入「后巷」的阴影中。
t_en: The man is borderline hysterical, raving manically as he vanishes back into the darkness of the "back alley."

t_ch: 他那因惊恐而颤抖不已的双眸给你留下了深刻印象。
t_en: Those eyes of his, quivering from sheer fear — they leave a lasting impression on you.

t_ch: （感觉有点可怕…不知道以后还会不会在这里遇到他。）
t_en: (This is all getting creepy... I wonder if I'll run into him again.)

t_ch: 又是那个男人——就是那个自称从「后巷」来、被某种怪异现象纠缠着的可怜男子——想必你还记得他。
t_en: It's that man again — the one who claimed he came from the "back alley," the poor fellow harassed by supernatural stuff. You remember him, surely.

t_ch: 他面容憔悴、双眼布满血丝，多半是太久没有得到充分休息了。即便如此，他仍背靠铁门喃喃自语，仿佛要将全身力气榨干一般咬牙切齿着。
t_en: His face is gaunt, his eyes bloodshot, most likely from a severe lack of much-needed rest. Even so, he leans against the iron gate, murmuring to himself, as if trying to exert every ounce of leftover strength through gritted teeth.

t_ch: 哈哈…它们发现不了我……
t_en: Haha... they'll never find me...

t_ch: 你还好吗？
t_en: Are you feeling okay?

t_ch: ——不要靠近我！
t_en: ...Get away from me!

t_ch: 噢，不好意思…我将您错认成……
t_en: Ohh, my apologies... I thought you were...

t_ch: …不，不，不。我怎么知道你不是那玩意？！别过来！
t_en: ...No, no. How do I know you're not one of them!? Get away!

t_ch: 啊…啊啊…杂乱的脚步声…还有那该死的笑声…！
t_en: Ah... Agh... those cluttered footsteps... and that blasted cackle...!

t_ch: 它们来了…它们来了…！
t_en: They're here... they're here...!

t_ch: <color=#eb4d3dff>轮到我了……</color>
t_en: <color=#eb4d3dff>It's my turn... </color>

t_ch: <color=#eb4d3dff>下一个…下一个就是我……</color>
t_en: <color=#eb4d3dff>I... I'm next... </color>

t_ch: 男人发出一阵刺耳的尖叫，狂笑着冲进了剧院背后那深不见底的「后巷」。
t_en: The man lets loose an ear-piercing shriek and charges into the unfathomable "back alley," carrying his maniacal laughter with him.

t_ch: 前无路
t_en: Dead end

t_ch: 「无限之雅努斯」托梦告诉过我，后面什么都没有……
t_en: "Janus the Endless" told me through a dream that there is nothing else that comes thereafter...

t_ch: 是真话喀。前面的区域，以后再来探索吧。
t_en: True that. How about we explore the area ahead of us later?

t_ch: 接受挑战吧——再巍峨的高山，也敌不过我的全力一击喀！
t_en: Get ready for a challenge: No matter how great a mountain, there's no way it can take my all-out assault!

t_ch: 你说这不是高山，只是持矛君王的雕塑？差点被它骗了喀！
t_en: Are you telling me that's not a mountain, but a statue of the Spearlord? You almost had me there!

t_ch: 看到了吧，再巍峨的高山，我轻轻一碰也就应声迸裂了喀！
t_en: See that? No matter how great the mountain, I can crack it open with a single flicker!

t_ch: 虽然，我也付出了惨痛的代价喀……
t_en: Despite the heavy price I had to pay...

t_ch: 温暖的篝火啊，散发你汹涌的火光，将一切黑暗驱散吧。
t_en: O warm bonfire: Spread your raging flames and dispel all the darkness.

t_ch: 呦吼吼吼吼…冒险者啊，一路上辛苦了喀。在篝火边休整休整再出发吧。
t_en: Yo ho ho... Adventurer, you've come a long way already. Let's rest beside the campfire and take off again later.

t_ch: 吾主昔日的警示仍在我的脑中回响：当立足的大地由红转蓝，前往彼岸的大门便将打开。
t_en: I keep playing back in my head my master's warning: The door to the other shore will only open once the ground beneath you turns from red to blue.

t_ch: 我站在这里，应该算是变蓝了喀？
t_en: I'm standing over here. That's blue, right?

t_ch: 吾主昔日的警示仍在我的脑中回响：当立足的大地由红转蓝，前往彼岸的大门便将打开。
t_en: I keep playing back in my head my master's warning: The door to the other shore will only open once the ground beneath you turns from red to blue.

t_ch: 我的使命，应该已经完成了喀？
t_en: My mission should now be complete, right?

t_ch: 墙上刻着的这家伙居然没有鼻子，岂不是得一直憋气，也太可怜了喀！
t_en: This wall carving here doesn't even have a nose. Don't tell me the poor guy has to hold his breath the whole time!

t_ch: 所以本虫就勉强先顶替一下，绝对不是在偷懒喀！
t_en: I guess my hand is forced, and I'll take over things from here. Absolutely no time for slacking off!

t_ch: 真正的硬汉不屑听拐弯抹角的诳语，更不屑走弯弯绕绕的歧路——干脆自己钻一条路喀！
t_en: A real tough guy doesn't listen to lies and gossip, and has even less time for beating around the bush — just carve out your own way, I say!

t_ch: 不过…持矛君王手下的工匠，用料也太扎实了！我的脑壳都嗡嗡响了喀！
t_en: However... The Spearlord's craftsman really gets to use the good stuff! My head's buzzing loud!

t_ch: 直视深渊，如同直视那位不可提及名字的霾中之影…究竟何时，它才愿将尘世的幸福归还于我们？
t_en: Gazing into the abyss is like staring into that hazy shadow, the one that must not be named... When will they finally return to us the joy of the mundane world?

t_ch: 吾主啊，惟愿你能如昔日一般将启示留在风中，送到我的耳畔喀。
t_en: O master, how I wish you could whisper those revelations into my ear, as you always used to.

t_ch: 在外漂泊的游子，一想到有温暖的港湾等着自己，心底都会踏实很多喀？
t_en: Travelers from afar always find themselves more at ease when they think of the safe harbor awaiting them, right?

t_ch: 但本虫暂住的这里连门都没有，一点也不温暖喀！
t_en: But the place I'm slumming at doesn't even have a front door — and it sure isn't warm at all!

t_ch: 时过境迁，悬锋暴君的塑像已成陈迹，苔藓将用它青绿的手抚去过往的一切辉煌和悲欢。
t_en: As time marches on, the statue of the tyrant of Kremnos has become a relic as green mossy fingers smother all the glory and tragedy of the past.

t_ch: 这莫非也是吾主的意志喀……
t_en: Could this also be the will of my master...?

t_ch: 喀！角斗太惊心动魄，我一连看了三天三夜，不小心睡着了喀！
t_en: Crrk! The fight was so thrilling I had to watch it back-to-back for three days straight — so I ended up falling asleep!

t_ch: 真是一场酣畅淋漓的比赛啊，都把天给看黑了喀！
t_en: It was such a rousing contest that I watched it until it got dark outside!

t_ch: 壁画里的这堆矿石堆放得也太不对称了，看得人心里痒痒的喀。
t_en: This pile of ore in the mural is offset. It's not symmetrical, and it's going to trigger people's urges.

t_ch: {RUBY_B#塔兰顿}行律者{RUBY_E#}绝对不会原谅此等不均之事！能不能扣几块下来喀？
t_en: {RUBY_B#Talanton}The ruler{RUBY_E#} would never let such unbalance go unpunished! Can I chisel a few pieces of this ore off?

t_ch: 据说只有真正的勇者，才能拔出这柄持矛君王首肯的长剑喀！可我已经竭尽全力，它还是纹丝不动……
t_en: Legend has it that only a true hero can pull out this longsword endorsed by the Spearlord themself! Try as I might, though, it won't budge an inch...

t_ch: 莫非我不是预言中的勇者喀？我不能接受喀！
t_en: Could it be that I am somehow not the hero of prophecy? I can't take it!

t_ch: 没想到躲在远离污浊的高处，也无法摆脱尘世的纷扰…事到如今，我只有一个请求。
t_en: Who knew that the grief and turmoil of the mortal world could not be shaken, even by hiding all the way up here in this filthy place... Yet here we are. I have but one request.

t_ch: 在用{RUBY_B#扎格列斯}两面窃贼{RUBY_E#}的脏手打招呼前，先洗洗手喀！
t_en: Could you please give your hands a wash before you offer a greeting using the filthy hand of {RUBY_B#Zagreus} — that two-faced thief{RUBY_E#}!

t_ch: 与亦敌亦友的同伴并肩杀敌，这是诗人们口述的长诗中才有的段落喀。
t_en: Fighting shoulder-to-shoulder alongside someone neither fully friend nor foe is the kind of detail you only read about in the epic odes passed down via the poets' word-of-mouth recitals.

t_ch: 没想到如今还能见到此种情景，我也要燃起来了喀！
t_en: I never thought I'd get to witness such a thing today. It's really getting me fired up!

t_ch: 在一个合格的工匠眼里，即便高墙倾塌、雕像坍圮，顽石里依然包孕着未来的模样。
t_en: In the eyes of a certified craftsman, even if the walls fall down and the statues collapse to the ground, the rubble will still contain the shape of the future.

t_ch: 决定了——就以太太的尊容为蓝本雕琢喀！
t_en: I've made up my mind — I'm using my lady's visage as the subject of this sculpture!

t_ch: 跋涉过千山万水，我终于来到了掌管铸造的祭坛喀！牢不可破的躯壳，请赐予我！攻无不克的勇力，请赐予我！
t_en: Finally — after traversing scores of mountains and rivers, at last I arrive at the foundry altar! Please, grant me an indestructible form! Grant me the courage to conquer all before me!

t_ch: 坚不可摧的意志…这就不必了，因为我早就已经有了喀~
t_en: There's no need to ask for an indomitable will... Because that's something I've always had~

t_ch: 据说这是军纪严明的悬锋城人最爱的游戏之一喀，当阵列变化，五面同样颜色的盾牌排成一列，就会发生奇妙的事情！
t_en: Apparently this game is a favorite of Castrum Kremnos' locals, who maintain strict military rigor. When a formation changes and five identical colors of shield line up, miraculous things can happen!

t_ch: 真的可以做到吗？我很怀疑喀！
t_en: Is that really possible, though? I seriously doubt it!

t_ch: 这柄「天谴之锋」，铭纹里仍涌动着持矛君王当年注入的神性。我好像感受到了…感受到了…感受到了——
t_en: The inscriptions etched into this Blade of Fury still pulse with the divinity that the Spearlord injected into it. I think I can feel it... Yes, I feel it... I feel it...

t_ch: 好吧，是错觉喀。
t_en: Okay, fine. It was just my imagination.

t_ch: 本虫愿舍弃灵智，为这副钢甲铸造灵魂，驱使它护卫在吾主和太太驾前！
t_en: I am hereby willing to sacrifice my wits to forge a soul for this armor, and drive it in defense of my master and my lady!

t_ch: 可惜这只是张壁画…唉，假如是真的该多好喀。
t_en: It's a real shame this is just a mural though... *sigh* How wonderful it would be were it real.

t_ch: 喀喀喀喀…前面有可怕的气息传来，像墨汁一样漆黑，满是杀戮的阴冷腥味……
t_en: Crrk crrk crrk... I'm getting a really terrifying vibe from up ahead. It's jet black with this pungent fishy death smell...

t_ch: 你、你们一定要小心喀！
t_en: Y—You have to be careful!

t_ch: 我感觉到了…悬锋城的勇士正在与门对面那可怕的力量抗衡，一次又一次倒下，又一次又一次站起来……
t_en: I can feel it... The warriors of Castrum Kremnos are locked in combat with that terrifying force behind the gates. One after the other, they fall down, and they get back up again...

t_ch: 我、我也要鼓起勇气，不能给吾主丢脸喀！
t_en: I... I need to pluck up my courage, so I don't bring shame unto my master!

t_ch: 向你挑战！
t_en: I challenge you!

t_ch: 不过如此…
t_en: Is this all...

t_ch: 温暖的篝火啊…
t_en: The warm bonfire...

t_ch: 没有反应…
t_en: No reaction...

t_ch: 使命完成了喀！
t_en: Mission accomplished, crrkk!

t_ch: 钻啊钻…
t_en: Burrow, burrow...

t_ch: 深渊啊…
t_en: O Abyss...

t_ch: 虫子应该呆在真正的家！
t_en: Nymphs should live in their true home!

t_ch: 时过境迁…
t_en: Times have changed...

t_ch: 呼噜…呼噜…
t_en: *gurgle*... *gurgle*...

t_ch: 吭哧吭哧！
t_en: Toil, toil away!

t_ch: 我不能接受！
t_en: I won't accept this!

t_ch: 燃起来了！
t_en: I'm all fired up!

t_ch: 吭哧吭哧…
t_en: Toil, toil away...

t_ch: 请赐予我！
t_en: Please grace me!

t_ch: 真伤脑筋…
t_en: How vexing...

t_ch: 我感受到了！
t_en: I can feel it!

t_ch: 我来组成头部！
t_en: I'll be the head!

t_ch: 好、好可怕！
t_en: S—Scary!

t_ch: 加、加油啊！
t_en: K—Keep it up!

t_ch: 我想挣点*外快*。你懂的。
t_en: I'm looking for some EXTRA DOUGH. You know what I mean?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你的意思是……
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you mean...

t_ch: 嗐，你得是有多绝望，才会想到找我要活干啊？
t_en: Heh. You must be pretty desperate if you are coming to me for work.

t_ch: 我倒是没什么意见，矿队向来不嫌人多——话说回来，你手上有干活的家伙吗？
t_en: I'm not judging. The mining team can never be too big. Anyway, you have tools to work with?

t_ch: 我有一双无情铁手。
t_en: I have two iron hands!

t_ch: 你这语气…好像不是在开玩笑？
t_en: The way you say that... you're being serious?

t_ch: 你看这棒球棍可以吗？
t_en: How about this baseball bat?

t_ch: 嗯，我见识过你这玩意的威力，硬度肯定够——不过你还得留着它揍人吧？
t_en: Hmm. I've seen this thing of yours in action. It definitely is solid, but shouldn't you hold on to it to fight?

t_ch: 没有。
t_en: No.

t_ch: 嚯，你这人倒是实诚，讨饭也不带个碗。
t_en: Heh. Aren't you just an honest soul? If you're gonna beg, you better bring a bowl.

t_ch: 一般情况，找我租借矿具是要交订金或者实物抵押的…不是我小气啊，是环境如此——不讲信誉的，贪小便宜的家伙可太多了。
t_en: Normally, I don't lend mining tools without a cash deposit or something to serve as collateral... This isn't me being stingy here. It's just the environment. There are way too many people out there who go back on their word and try to nickle and dime me.

t_ch: 不过我信得过你。喏，我手边正好有一把八成新的二手矿镐，你拿去用吧。
t_en: That said. I do trust you. Hmm. I just so happen to have a mostly new, second-hand pickax. Take it.

t_ch: 大矿区那片巨大的地髓矿脉，你还有印象吧？我建议你先去那儿试试手。哦对了，要是挖到什么有意思的东西，别忘了拿来给我估估价。
t_en: You still remember that massive Geomarrow in the Great Mine? I suggest you head out that way first and try your luck. Oh, right. If you dig up anything interesting, don't forget to bring it to me for an appraisal.

t_ch: 加油吧，新晋矿工{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Good luck, {NICKNAME} the rookie miner.

t_ch: 你站在这里，那一大片地髓矿脉也站在这里。你们四——唔，也可能是五，三十七或一千六百五十八——目相对，沉默不语。
t_en: You stand here together with the vast Geomarrow vein. The two of you, or maybe five... or thirty-seven... or one thousand six hundred and fifty-eight of you look at each other without a word.

t_ch: 它雄伟，温暖，柔情。想想看，它能为成千上万的贝洛伯格居民带去维系生命的能源…它将从饥寒交加的孤夜手里拯救多少个家庭，守护多少孩童的笑容……
t_en: The Geomarrow is grandiose, warm, and gentle. Just think about how it provides life-sustaining energy to tens of thousands of Belobog citizens... How many families it saved from the lonely nights full of cold and hunger, and how many children's smiles it had protected...

t_ch: 矿脉一言不发，它的伟岸已经足够令你落泪——
t_en: The vein is silent. Its greatness is already enough to move you to tears.

t_ch: ——但还不至于让你忘记自己此行的目的：贪婪的开拓者，不惜跟矿工和流浪者抢夺饭碗，只为了搞些零花钱。
t_en: But it's not enough to make you forget your goal. You are the greedy Trailblazer who is taking jobs away from miners and vagrants, so long as it makes you some pocket money.

t_ch: 你从隐形的背包中掏出安东尼娜送给你的矿镐。该你上场了，新晋矿工{NICKNAME}。
t_en: From your invisible bag, you pull out the pickax Antonia gave you. It's time to get down to business, {NICKNAME} the rookie miner.

t_ch: 用尽全身力气敲下去。
t_en: Strike as hard as you can.

t_ch: 哐！
t_en: Clank!

t_ch: 橙色、黑色和白色的碎片散落在你的脚下。只有橙色的那几片属于你眼前的矿脉。
t_en: Shards of orange, black, and white scatter around your feet. Only the orange shards belong to the vein that you see before you.

t_ch: 很显然，你搞砸了——八成新的热动力矿镐，只在一瞬间就变成了没人看得上眼的报废垃圾。
t_en: It's plain as day that you have broken the "<unbreak>80%</unbreak> New Thermal Mining Pickaxe." It devolved into worthless trash in an instant.

t_ch: 你抬头看着那片雄伟的矿脉…橙色的面容缓和慈祥，但你还是觉察到了它藏在表面之下的嘲弄：
t_en: You look up at the majestic vein... The orange surface has a warmth and kindness to it, but you still notice the mockery beneath its surface.

t_ch: 「干得好，小东西。」
t_en: "Well done, little one."

t_ch: 你羞恼不已。你打算再去找一次那位女工头…无论如何，这个场子必须由你亲自找回来。
t_en: You feel extreme embarrassment. You plan to go back to the supervisor... No matter what, you have to personally regain your honor.

t_ch: 用*十分*适中的力气敲下去。
t_en: Strike with a VERY moderate amount of force.

t_ch: 真的吗？你真的是如此无趣的{F#女人}{M#男人}吗？
t_en: Really? Are you really such a boring {F#woman}{M#man}?

t_ch: 你明明还有其它选择…难道就这么甘于平庸吗？你难道就不好奇，那些看似不合情理的选择背后埋藏了多么美妙的意外吗？
t_en: You have other choices... Are you really willing to settle for something so average? Aren't you the least bit curious about what amazing surprises lurk behind unreasonable choices?

t_ch: 中也者，天下之大本也。
t_en: Balance is the very foundation of the world.

t_ch: ——你已经做好决定了，没人能说服你改变想法。挥镐吧，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: You've made your choice. No one is going to change your mind. Get that pickax moving, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 噹！
t_en: Dong!

t_ch: ——赞美琥珀王！你从来没干过类似的活，但这一击的力度简直完美如梦境…也许你天生就是干这行的料。
t_en: Praise the Amber Lord! You have never done anything like this, but you used the absolutely perfect amount of strength... Maybe you have found your life calling.

t_ch: 一块拳头大小的地髓结晶落在你的脚边。它的形状近似正十二面体，颜色橙红剔透，核芯周围甚至还反射出几抹靛蓝。
t_en: A Geomarrow crystal the size of your fist falls next to your feet. It has a shape similar to a dodecahedron with a clear, orange-red color. There are even some shocks of indigo that radiate from the core of the stone.

t_ch: 不需要冗杂的鉴定步骤，你内心已经十分清楚——这下发财了。
t_en: A stone like this doesn't need complex appraisal. You know full well that this is big money.

t_ch: 你抬头看着那片雄伟的矿脉…橙色的面容缓和慈祥。仔细聆听…它似乎对这次完美的挥镐发表了自己的看法。
t_en: You look up to gaze at the magnificent vein... Its orange surface is warm and kind. If you listen closely... you can almost hear the vein give its own take on your perfect swing.

t_ch: 「干得好，小东西。」
t_en: "Well done, little one."

t_ch: 你对着自然的伟大显像轻鞠一躬。现在，回去找那个女工头，让她帮忙鉴定这块地髓结晶的价值吧。
t_en: You give a slight bow to this marvel of nature. Now, it's time to go back to the supervisor and have her appraise how much this Geomarrow crystal is worth.

t_ch: 重新选择…做个有趣的人。
t_en: Make a different decision... be interesting!

t_ch: 轻轻地敲下去。
t_en: Gently tap downward.

t_ch: 叮！
t_en: Ding!
"Ding"?

t_ch: 然后呢？
t_en: Go on.

t_ch: 「叮」——这就是全部了，没有下文。你在期待些什么？
t_en: "Ding." That's it. There's nothing else. What were you expecting?

t_ch: 你抬头看着那片雄伟的矿脉…橙色的面容缓和慈祥，但你还是觉察到了它藏在表面之下的嘲弄：
t_en: You look up at the majestic vein... The orange surface has a warmth and kindness to it, but you still notice the mockery beneath its surface.

t_ch: 「没吃饭吗，小东西？」
t_en: "You didn't eat a good meal, little one?"

t_ch: 你羞恼不已。再给你一次机会，你一定会给它点颜色瞧瞧。
t_en: You are embarrassed. If you are given a second chance, you'll teach it a lesson.

t_ch: 额…有关挣外快的那件事……
t_en: Umm... So about making some extra dough...

t_ch: 哦——这事啊，我记得呢。怎么样，{NICKNAME}？你的矿工生涯还顺利吗？
t_en: Oh. That. Yeah, I remember. What about it, {NICKNAME}? Everything going well with your mining gig?

t_ch: 出了一点小意外…
t_en: Something kind of unexpected happened...

t_ch: …意外？
t_en: ...Unexpected?

t_ch: 哦…我大概明白了。我猜猜，你把矿镐弄坏了，是吧？
t_en: Oh... I think I understand. Let me guess, you broke the pickax, right?

t_ch: 我把你借我的装备弄坏了。
t_en: I broke the pickax you lent me.

t_ch: …啊？这才没过半天——
t_en: ...Huh? You've barely been at it for half a day —

t_ch: ——咳，算了，把专业设备就这么交给彻头彻尾的外行，我也有责任……
t_en: Ahem. Anyway. I suppose I'm partly to blame. I did give professional equipment to an absolute noobie.

t_ch: 我猜猜…有的人还不死心，还想再找我借一次装备，是吧？
t_en: Let me guess... Someone isn't going to take no for an answer and wants me to lend them equipment again. Is that right?

t_ch: 被你猜中了。
t_en: You are right on the money.

t_ch: 古人云，事不过三…
t_en: They say the third time's the charm.

t_ch: …明白了，那我就再信你一回。我这儿还有一把闲置的矿镐，你拿去吧。
t_en: ...I get it. I'll trust you one more time. I have one more pickax lying around. Take it.

t_ch: 不过，咳，这回我得收点押金…希望你能理解。
t_en: But, ahem, I will be taking a deposit... I hope you can understand.

t_ch: 只收你五百信用点，怎么样？
t_en: How about just <unbreak>500</unbreak> credits?

t_ch: 我不会辜负你的。
t_en: I won't disappoint you.

t_ch: 好，拿去吧。记得这次要好好爱惜装备。
t_en: Alright, alright. Just take it. Remember to take good care of the equipment.

t_ch: 你从安东尼娜手中接过矿镐。如果说上次给你的设备有八成新…那这次顶多只有六成。
t_en: You take the pickax from Antonia's hands. If the last piece of equipment was eighty-percent new... This one was just about sixty-percent new.

t_ch: …失去的两成是女矿头对你的失望。
t_en: ...The drop consists of her disappointment in you.

t_ch: 让我再考虑一下…
t_en: Let me think about it...

t_ch: 看来你是真的缺钱…行吧，你再考虑考虑，我就在这儿等你。
t_en: Looks like you're really hard up for cash. Give it some thought. I'll be here waiting for you.

t_ch: 你听说了吗？
t_en: Have you heard the news?

t_ch: 冷面小青龙…
t_en: Cold Dragon Young...

t_ch: 嗯，听说了。
t_en: Oh, yeah. I heard.

t_ch: 居然宣布退役了…
t_en: I can't believe he announced his retirement...

t_ch: 不敢相信…
t_en: Doesn't seem real...

t_ch: 他的实力绝对够争冠军。
t_en: He was so good. Definitely could have become the champion.

t_ch: 激流勇退啊…
t_en: To bow out when you're at your prime...

t_ch: 佩服，真是佩服。
t_en: Admirable. Truly admirable.

t_ch: 真可惜啊，我本来以为「冷面小青龙」能席卷下层区，成为搏击界的超级新星……
t_en: What a shame though. I thought for sure that Cold Dragon Young could sweep the Underworld and become the new superstar of the fighting world...

t_ch: 嗯，我当时也有同感。虽然他只上场了三四次，但几乎每一局都是碾压获胜——连一滴汗都不带流的。
t_en: Uh-huh. I feel the same way as you. He might have only gotten into the ring three or four times, but he crushed his opponent every match without breaking a sweat.

t_ch: 悄悄告诉你吧，我觉得这肯定是什么营销手段！你想啊，史考特那老家伙怎么可能放弃这种摇钱树？
t_en: Let me tell you something. Just between us, I think this must be some kind of marketing tactic! Think about it. How is Scott just going to let this money tree get away from him?

t_ch: 要我说，「冷面小青龙」以后肯定会回归的。到时候，他的周边商品价格肯定大涨——这都是无良商人骗钱的套路！
t_en: The way I see it, Cold Dragon Young is going to return for sure, and when he does, the price of his merchandise is going to jump up. That's how those grubby merchants scam people!

t_ch: 你说的有点道理。我要是能搞到「冷面小青龙」的签名就好了……
t_en: You've got a point. If only I could get my hands on Cold Dragon Young's autograph...

t_ch: 或者，想得再大胆点…要是能拿到他的签名照，咱们以后绝对能发一笔大财。
t_en: Or, even better... If we could get my hands on a signed photograph of him, we would be rolling in money, <unbreak>100%</unbreak>.

t_ch: （这两个人好像对「冷面小青龙」的周边很感兴趣……）
t_en: (These two seem pretty interested in Cold Dragon Young's merch...)

t_ch: （给丹恒发个消息吧。）
t_en: (Let's send a message to Dan Heng.)

t_ch: 我给你们看个宝贝。
t_en: I'll give you a look at my treasure here.

t_ch: …啊？您哪位啊？
t_en: ...Huh? Who are you?

t_ch: 我们认识你吗？
t_en: Do we know you?
